revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: 新しいページ：矢藤治山古墳（岡山県岡山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 矢藤治山古墳
（矢藤治山墳丘墓） |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 34.56 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長35.5m
高さ3m（後円部） |築造年代 = |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]1基
　　　（内部に箱形木棺）
前方部：1基 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品・[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]] |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 矢藤治山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''矢藤治山古墳'''（やとうじやまこふん）または'''矢藤治山墳丘墓'''（やとうじやまふんきゅうぼ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻にある[[古墳]]（または[[墳丘墓]]）。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山市街地から西方、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の南面の尾根上に築造された古墳（または墳丘墓）である史跡説明板。。[[1990年|1990]]-[[1992年]]（[[平成]]2-4年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部がバチ形に開く前方後円形で、前方部を南方に向ける。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]（または特殊器台形埴輪）が検出されている。主体部の埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部にそれぞれ1基が存在する。後円部のものは[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に箱形木棺が据えられており、調査では副葬品が検出されている。 この矢藤治山古墳は、[[弥生時代]]終末期・[[古墳時代]]最初期の築造と推定される。最古型式の前方後円墳として、弥生時代から古墳時代への墓制の変遷を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。 * 墳丘長：約35.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約23.5メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：約11.8メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が存在する。石室規模は長さ2.7メートル、幅1.0メートル、高さ0.7メートル。石室内部には北を頭位とする箱形木棺が据えられたと見られ、痕跡から木棺の大きさは長さ約2.5メートル・幅約0.85メートルと推測される。この石室からは後述の副葬品が検出されている。 このほかの埋葬施設として、前方部においても木棺の痕跡が認められているが、詳細は明らかでない。 == 出土品 == '''後円部埋葬施設の副葬品''' * 棺内 ** 方格規矩鏡 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** ガラス小玉 50 * 棺外 ** 鉄斧 1 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988年}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 矢藤治山古墳
（矢藤治山墳丘墓） |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 34.56 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長35.5m

　　　（内部に箱形木棺）
前方部：1基 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品・[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]] |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 矢藤治山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''矢藤治山古墳'''（やとうじやまこふん）または'''矢藤治山墳丘墓'''（やとうじやまふんきゅうぼ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻にある[[古墳]]（または[[墳丘墓]]）。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山市街地から西方、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の南面の尾根上に築造された古墳（または墳丘墓）である史跡説明板。。[[1990年|1990]]-[[1992年]]（[[平成]]2-4年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部がバチ形に開く前方後円形で、前方部を南方に向ける。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]（または特殊器台形埴輪）が検出されている。主体部の埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部にそれぞれ1基が存在する。後円部のものは[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に箱形木棺が据えられており、調査では副葬品が検出されている。 この矢藤治山古墳は、[[弥生時代]]終末期-[[古墳時代]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。最古型式の前方後円墳として、弥生時代から古墳時代への墓制の変遷を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。 * 墳丘長：約35.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約23.5メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：約11.8メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が存在する。石室規模は長さ2.7メートル、幅1.0メートル、高さ0.7メートル。石室内部には北を頭位とする箱形木棺が据えられたと見られ、痕跡から木棺の大きさは長さ約2.5メートル・幅約0.85メートルと推測される。この石室からは後述の副葬品が検出されている。 このほかの埋葬施設として、前方部においても木棺の痕跡が認められているが、詳細は明らかでない。 == 出土品 == '''後円部埋葬施設の副葬品''' * 棺内 ** 方格規矩鏡 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** ガラス小玉 50 * 棺外 ** 鉄斧 1 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=矢藤治山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

　　　（内部に箱形木棺）
前方部：1基 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品・[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]] |史跡 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 矢藤治山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''矢藤治山古墳'''（やとうじやまこふん）または'''矢藤治山墳丘墓'''（やとうじやまふんきゅうぼ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻にある[[古墳]]（または[[墳丘墓]]）。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山市街地から西方、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の南面の尾根上に築造された古墳（または墳丘墓）である史跡説明板。。[[1990年|1990]]-[[1992年]]（[[平成]]2-4年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部がバチ形に開く前方後円形で、前方部を南方に向ける。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]（または特殊器台形埴輪）が検出されている。埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部にそれぞれ1基が存在する。後円部のものは[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に箱形木棺が据えられており、調査では副葬品が検出されている。 この矢藤治山古墳は、[[弥生時代]]終末期-[[古墳時代]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。最古型式の前方後円墳として、弥生時代から古墳時代への墓制の変遷を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳]（岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」）。。 * 墳丘長：約35.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約23.5メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：約11.8メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が存在する。石室規模は長さ2.7メートル、幅1.0メートル、高さ0.7メートル。石室内部には北を頭位とする箱形木棺が据えられたと見られ、痕跡から木棺の大きさは長さ約2.5メートル・幅約0.85メートルと推測される。この石室からは後述の副葬品が検出されている。 このほかの埋葬施設として、前方部においても木棺の痕跡が認められているが、詳細は明らかでない。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|方格規矩鏡}}{{small|岡山大学考古学研究室所蔵。岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}}]] '''後円部埋葬施設の副葬品''' * 棺内 ** [[方格規矩鏡]] 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** ガラス小玉 50 * 棺外 ** 鉄斧 1 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=矢藤治山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=矢藤治山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市「岡山シティミュージアム」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{Infobox Ancient Japanese Burial Mound |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters * Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: * Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:Yatouji-yama Yayoi Burial Hōkaku Kiku-kyō.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: * {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 * Magatama: 1 * Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: * Iron axe: 1 Yatōji-yama Burial Magatama.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[category:new pages]][[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Infobox Ancient Japanese Burial Mound |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters * Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: * Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:Yatouji-yama Yayoi Burial Hōkaku Kiku-kyō.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: * {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 * Magatama: 1 * Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: * Iron axe: 1 Yatōji-yama Burial Magatama.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters * Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: * Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:Yatouji-yama Yayoi Burial Hōkaku Kiku-kyō.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: * {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 * Magatama: 1 * Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: * Iron axe: 1 Yatōji-yama Burial Magatama.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters * Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: * Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:Yatouji-yama Yayoi Burial Hōkaku Kiku-kyō.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: * {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 * Magatama: 1 * Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: * Iron axe: 1 Yatōji-yama Burial Magatama.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatōji Yama Kofun}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Tumulus |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |artifacts = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Tumulus''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
