{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |burial_facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = |Burial Facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |Burial Facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |Burial Facility = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation" Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. Artifacts found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == Artifacts == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] Artifacts discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m

Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designated as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 39|latitude_seconds = 34.56 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == Burial Facilities == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The dimensions of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinates_degrees = 34|coordinates_minutes = 39|coordinates_seconds = 34.56 |coordinates_degrees = 133|coordinatesutes = 51|coordinatesonds = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m

Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed information is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |other_names = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinatesonds = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not other_designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not other_designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not other_designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not other_designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation" Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not other_designation1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's other_designation"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory.
