revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yatōjiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōjiyama Kofun}}

Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yatōjiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōjiyama Kofun}}

The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=draft|12=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yatōji Yama Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yatōjiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatōjiyama Kofun}}

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:矢藤治山古墳 up to rev 101786614
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 矢藤治山古墳 / 矢藤治山墳丘墓 |画像 = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
墳丘入り口 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 34.56 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長35.5m
高さ3m（後円部） |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に木棺）
前方部：1基 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 方格規矩鏡・勾玉・ガラス小玉・鉄斧・[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]] |史跡 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 矢藤治山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''矢藤治山古墳'''（やとうじやまこふん）または'''矢藤治山墳丘墓'''（やとうじやまふんきゅうぼ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻にある[[古墳]]（または[[墳丘墓]]）。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の南面の尾根上に築造された古墳（または墳丘墓）である史跡説明板。。[[1990年|1990]]-[[1992年]]（[[平成]]2-4年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部がバチ形に開く前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]（または特殊器台形埴輪）が検出されている。埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部にそれぞれ1基が存在する。後円部のものは[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に箱形木棺が据えられる。石室内の調査では副葬品として方格規矩鏡・勾玉・ガラス小玉・鉄斧が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[弥生時代]]終末期-[[古墳時代]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。最古型式の前方後円墳として、弥生時代から古墳時代への墓制の変遷を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。 * 墳丘長：約35.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約23.5メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：約11.8メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。石室規模は長さ2.7メートル・幅1.0メートル・高さ0.7メートル。石室内部には北を頭位とする箱形木棺が据えられたと見られ、痕跡から木棺の大きさは長さ約2.5メートル・幅約0.85メートルと推測される。 このほかの埋葬施設として、前方部においても木棺の痕跡が認められているが、詳細は明らかでない。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|方格規矩鏡}}{{small|岡山大学考古学研究室所蔵。岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 後円部埋葬施設から検出された副葬品は次の通り。 * 棺内 ** [[方格規矩鏡]] 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** ガラス小玉 50 * 棺外 ** 鉄斧 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|勾玉
{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=矢藤治山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[矢藤治山古墳]] to [[Yatōjiyama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 矢藤治山古墳 / 矢藤治山墳丘墓 |画像 = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
墳丘入り口 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 34.56 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 35.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長35.5m
高さ3m（後円部） |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に木棺）
前方部：1基 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 方格規矩鏡・勾玉・ガラス小玉・鉄斧・[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]] |史跡 = なし |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Okayama |ラベル = 矢藤治山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''矢藤治山古墳'''（やとうじやまこふん）または'''矢藤治山墳丘墓'''（やとうじやまふんきゅうぼ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]西花尻・東花尻にある[[古墳]]（または[[墳丘墓]]）。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の南面の尾根上に築造された古墳（または墳丘墓）である史跡説明板。。[[1990年|1990]]-[[1992年]]（[[平成]]2-4年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部がバチ形に開く前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]（または特殊器台形埴輪）が検出されている。埋葬施設は、後円部・前方部にそれぞれ1基が存在する。後円部のものは[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に箱形木棺が据えられる。石室内の調査では副葬品として方格規矩鏡・勾玉・ガラス小玉・鉄斧が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[弥生時代]]終末期-[[古墳時代]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。最古型式の前方後円墳として、弥生時代から古墳時代への墓制の変遷を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。 * 墳丘長：約35.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約23.5メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：約11.8メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。石室規模は長さ2.7メートル・幅1.0メートル・高さ0.7メートル。石室内部には北を頭位とする箱形木棺が据えられたと見られ、痕跡から木棺の大きさは長さ約2.5メートル・幅約0.85メートルと推測される。 このほかの埋葬施設として、前方部においても木棺の痕跡が認められているが、詳細は明らかでない。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|方格規矩鏡}}{{small|岡山大学考古学研究室所蔵。岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 後円部埋葬施設から検出された副葬品は次の通り。 * 棺内 ** [[方格規矩鏡]] 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** ガラス小玉 50 * 棺外 ** 鉄斧 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|勾玉
{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（岡山市吉備地区地域活性化推進実行委員会、1996年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=西花尻村|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|西花尻村（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=矢藤治山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|矢藤治山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html 矢藤治山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{DEFAULTSORT:やとうしやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|qq=}}, magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-ku (Okayama)|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|qq=}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|qq=}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-ku (Okayama)|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|qq=}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|qq=}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound entrance |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]] [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]] Nishihanajiri and Higashihanajiri |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = 34.56 |coordinatesrees = 133|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 35.87 |omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]

The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[en:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[ja:%E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]
