{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28685136]] Wikidata name: Yatōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = {{Coord|34.6596|N|133.859964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]][[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] [[sn:E7%9F%A2%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%BB%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] [[Category:Qq 4]] [[sn:Yatōjiyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}}

Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}}

A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Yatojiyama Kofun]][[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28685136]] Wikidata name: Yatōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = {{Coord|34.6596|N|133.859964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yatojiyama Kofun]][[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28685136]] Wikidata name: Yatōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = {{Coord|34.6596|N|133.859964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yatojiyama Kofun]][[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28685136]] Wikidata name: Yatōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = {{Coord|34.6596|N|133.859964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama

[[commons:Category:Yatojiyama Kofun]][[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yatojiyama Kofun]][[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28685136]] Wikidata name: Yatōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yatōji-yama Kofun / Yatōji-yama Kofun |image = File:Yatouji-yama Kofun, entrance.jpg |alternate_name = |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 39|coordinates = {{Coord|34.6596|N|133.859964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 35.5 m
Height: 3 m (rear circular section) |built = |burial = Rear circular section: {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a wooden coffin)
Front rectangular section: One facility |buried_person = |burial goods = [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, iron axe, {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} |historic_site_status = None |noteworthy_features = |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Yatōji-yama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (or tumulus) located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]], magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] through the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** [[:en:TLV mirror|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō]] (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}} == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Site explanation board (Kibi District Regional Revitalization Promotion Committee, installed in 1996). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nishihanajiri Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|Nishihanajiri Village (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Yatōji-yama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yatojiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|矢藤治山古墳|version=101786614|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] {{Commons category|Yatojiyama Kofun}} [[ja:矢藤治山古墳]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q28685136}}
