Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山古墳) or '''Yatōji-yama Kofun''' (矢藤治山墳丘墓) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (or tumulus) located in {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|1=北區}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic monument. == Overview == The Yatōji-yama Kofun, constructed on the southern slopes of the "Kibi no Nakayama" ridge, is located in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture.Site explanation board. Excavations were conducted between 1990 and 1992 (Heisei 2–4). The mound has a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south. The exterior includes paving stones and {{ill|Tokusutsuki|ja|特殊器台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusutsuki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}} or similar ceremonial items, possibly resembling haniwa. The burial facilities include one structure in the rear circular section and another in the front rectangular section. The rear circular section houses a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a box-shaped wooden coffin. burial goods found in the stone chamber include a {{ill|TLV mirror|en|TLV mirror|WD=Q3316102|lt=Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|1=TLV mirror}}, magatama, small glass beads, and an iron axe. The tumulus is estimated to date to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.{{Sfn|Yatōji-yama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} As one of the earliest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli, the Yatōji-yama Kofun is regarded as critical to understanding the transition from Yayoi burial practices to those of the Kofun period. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/08.html Yatōji-yama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). * Mound length: Approx. 35.5 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: Approx. 23.5 meters ** Height: Approx. 3 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: Approx. 11.8 meters == burial == The central burial facility in the rear circular section consists of a {{ill|Tateanashiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The area of the stone chamber are approximately 2.7 meters long, 1.0 meter wide, and 0.7 meters high. A box-shaped wooden coffin, aligned northward, is thought to have been placed inside. Traces suggest the coffin was about 2.5 meters long and 0.85 meters wide. A burial facility in the front rectangular section also showed traces of a wooden coffin, though detailed intypeation is unavailable. == burial goods == [[File:矢藤治山弥生墳丘墓出土 方格規矩鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hōkaku Kiku-kyō}}{{small|Property of Okayama University Archaeological Research Laboratory. Photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}]]] burial goods discovered from the rear circular section burial facility include: * Inside the coffin: ** {{ill|TLV mirror|en|TLV mirror|WD=Q3316102|lt=Hōkaku Kiku-kyō|1=TLV mirror}} (square-mirror with patterns): 1 ** Magatama: 1 ** Small glass beads: 50 * Outside the coffin: ** Iron axe: 1 矢藤治山古墳出土 勾玉.JPG|Magatama
